summary: Read a custom kind's units in `check` from a generic surface loader keyed on each kind's `governs.root`, replacing the `root == "specs"` special case that reads `Workspace.specs`.

edit.description: Add `Unit::from_surface_dir(dir)` — reload a written custom-unit surface (`<root>/<name>/{meta.toml, <BODY>.md}`) into a raw `Unit`: id = the surface dir name, body = the lone non-`meta.toml` `.md` sibling (byte-faithful), `source_path` read back from the `[provenance]` table. The generic reader the `check` custom-kind path uses in place of the built-in `Spec::from_surface_dir`; an unknown/malformed surface surfaces as a `KindError`.

Rewrite `custom_units` to load each declared custom kind's units generically from the workspace directory at its `governs.root` (each surface dir → `Unit` via `Unit::from_surface_dir`), instead of the `root == "specs"` special case reading `ws.specs`. Delete `spec_unit` and drop `use temper::spec::Spec` — the check path stops depending on the built-in `Spec` IR. Closes the 'extend the loader to every custom root' follow-on the special case flagged, so a custom kind rooted anywhere (not just `specs/`) is read.

tests.asserts: `Unit::from_surface_dir` reloads a written custom-unit surface into a raw `Unit` (id, byte-faithful body, provenance) for any `governs.root`, and the custom-kind `check` path reads its units through it rather than the built-in `Workspace.specs`.

acceptance: The custom-kind `check` path loads its units generically from the workspace at each kind's `governs.root` via `Unit::from_surface_dir`; `main.rs::custom_units` no longer reads `Workspace.specs` or the built-in `Spec` IR, a custom kind rooted outside `specs/` is now read, and the suite is green.
notes: Splits the built-in-spec retirement into disjoint, low-risk ticks: the generic reader ships first — green while `Spec`/`Workspace.specs` still exist but go unread by `check` (drift still reads them until RETIRE) — leaving RETIRE near-pure deletion. Realizes 40-composition's 'temper reads its own `specs/` because its `temper.toml` declares the kind, not because anything is hardwired.'

summary: Retire the built-in `spec` kind (`src/spec.rs`, `extract::spec_features`, `Workspace.specs`, `discover_spec_files`, drift's spec axis) — the data-declared `spec` kind now covers it.

per.section: Decision: a custom kind is declared data, never engine code

edit.description: Remove `Workspace.specs`, the `Spec::from_surface_dir` load in `Workspace::load`, `WorkspaceError::Spec`, `use crate::spec`, and the spec-load tests. Surface loading keeps only the built-in `skills`/`rules`; custom-unit reading is generic (KIND-CUSTOM-UNIT-LOADER's `Unit::from_surface_dir`), not spec-special.
Drop `spec_features` and its unit test, the `spec_in` test helper, and `use crate::spec::Spec` — a custom kind extracts via the data algebra (`custom.extraction.extract`), not a bespoke projector.

Remove the built-in spec drift axis: the `specs` block in `diff` (which reads `workspace.specs` + `import::discover_spec_files`), the `"spec"` `DriftEntry` kind it emits, and the spec drift tests. Generic custom-kind drift is future work alongside `apply`, not part of this retirement (scope cut, stated out loud).

Drop `discover_spec_files` (drift was its only caller) and reconcile the one import test that reloads a written spec surface via `crate::spec::Spec::from_surface_dir` to a generic surface reload — production import already writes custom units generically (`import_custom_unit`) and depends on no `Spec`. (There is no `import_spec`.)
Reconcile any end-to-end assertion referencing the built-in spec surface/IR to the data-declared custom-kind path.

tests.asserts: `temper` reads `specs/` solely through the data-declared custom kind — the built-in `Spec` IR, `spec_features`, `Workspace.specs`, drift's spec axis, and `discover_spec_files` are gone and the suite stays green.

acceptance: The built-in `spec` module and its bespoke plumbing (the `Spec` IR, `spec_features`, `Workspace.specs`, `discover_spec_files`, drift's spec axis) are removed; `temper` reads `specs/` only through the data-declared custom kind via the generic loader; the whole suite is green.
notes: Depends on KIND-CUSTOM-UNIT-LOADER (the generic reader) — after it this is near-pure deletion: `main.rs` already dropped its `Spec` use, so no main.rs edit here. A custom kind shipped as a built-in breaks 'temper ships none of them' (15-kinds Decision): a stranger installing temper would inherit temper's `spec` kind. Confirm no `check`/drift compile or test regression once `Spec` is gone.

summary: Reconcile the top-level `[[edge]]` construct into `[kind.<name>.relationships]` so a reference is a kind capability, not a separate construct.

per.section: The harness is a graph too — and references are declared edges

edit.description: Move edge parsing from the standalone top-level `[[edge]]` array to `[kind.<name>.relationships]` under the owning kind — the `<name>` is the edge source (the implicit `from`), and each relationship names its reference `field` and target `to` kind. Parse it for both a built-in-layer `[kind.<k>]` and a full custom-kind `[kind.<name>]` (relationships are orthogonal to the custom-vs-layer split). Preserve the parsed `Edge { field, from, to }` shape the graph consumes; drop `parse_edges`/`parse_edge`, the `[[edge]]` reader, and `EdgeRootNotArray`/`BadEdge`.
Read the declared relationships off the owning kinds rather than a free-standing edge list; route resolution + admissibility behavior unchanged (still over `Edge { field, from, to }`).
Assemble the harness graph from the kinds' relationships instead of `AuthorLayer::edges()`.
Update the edge-construction fixtures to the `[kind.<name>.relationships]` shape.

tests.asserts: Edges declared under `[kind.<name>.relationships]` assemble the harness graph and drive route resolution + admissibility exactly as the standalone `[[edge]]` construct did.

acceptance: A reference is declared under its owning kind's `[kind.<name>.relationships]` (over that kind's declared reference syntax) rather than a standalone `[[edge]]` table; the harness graph, route resolution, and edge-admissibility behavior are preserved.
notes: Note (d) of the reconciliation. The graph foundation shipped soundly (`src/graph.rs`, 54eb52f) over `AuthorLayer::edges`; this aligns the edge-declaration *shape* with the specced kind capability (15-kinds 'The entity graph is a kind capability'). `degree`/`acyclic` and the spec kind's references-resolve clause read the same relationships and follow as later entries.
